key_insight: A SCAPS-1D plus machine-learning workflow can rapidly rank multilayer perovskite solar-cell stacks and pinpoint the most important design knobs: HTL band gap, absorber band gap, and ETL electron affinity.
engineering_relevance: The approach shortens the search for high-efficiency, lead-free perovskite architectures by guiding material selection before expensive full-device simulation or experiments.
method: A representative subset trained an ML model to predict PCE for untested stacks.
SHAP analysis identified the dominant physical descriptors controlling performance.
evidence_signal: Leave-One-Group-Out cross-validation showed strong rank-prediction ability, and the top ML-suggested devices were confirmed by full SCAPS-1D simulations up to 28.85% PCE.
watch_item: The model is only as reliable as the simulated training space, so extrapolation to unseen materials or fabrication-dependent losses may be limited.
paper_title: Multilayer Screening of Double and Conventional Perovskite Solar Cells Using SCAPS-1D and Machine Learning: Optimization of ETL, HTL, and Absorber for High-Efficiency Architectures
